---
title: "Mixed Models for Repeated Measures (MMRM)"
---

::: {.callout-tip}
## This chapter is a stub — contributions welcome
Fill it in following the two full exemplars
([Summary Statistics](summary-statistics.qmd),
[ANCOVA](ancova.qmd)) and the
[topic template](https://github.com/OWNER/submission-transparency/blob/main/templates/topic-template.qmd).
See the checklist in [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](../CONTRIBUTING.md).
:::

## What a reviewer wants

For a longitudinal continuous endpoint, the adjusted treatment difference **at
the final planned visit**, from a model with an unstructured covariance over
time and no imputation of missing outcomes (MMRM is the pre-specified primary
analysis for many confirmatory trials).

## Suggested minimal approach

- **Fit:** the `mmrm` package —
  `mmrm(CHG ~ TRT * VISIT + BASE * VISIT + us(VISIT | USUBJID), data = adam)`.
  It is purpose-built, uses Satterthwaite/Kenward-Roger d.f., and is the common
  choice in the R-in-pharma community. `nlme::gls` with `corSymm` is a base-ish
  alternative worth showing side by side.
- **Report:** `emmeans(fit, ~ TRT | VISIT)` and contrast at the last visit.

## Simulation to build

- Simulate multivariate-normal repeated measures with a **known** visit-specific
  treatment effect and a realistic within-subject correlation, then induce MAR
  dropout.
- Confirm the last-visit treatment difference is unbiased and covers at ~95%
  under MAR, and contrast with a naive complete-case or LOCF analysis to show
  the bias those introduce.
